# BUG: when the destination sink already exists (created by a prior
# sflit move), subsequent moves into the same sink drop the doc
# comments of the newly-moved decls. The first move's doc is preserved;
# every move after it loses its leading // comment block.
#
# Hit repeatedly while splitting internal/ast/ast.go, internal/emit/
# emit.go, internal/infer/infer.go in the gojure project. After 6 splits
# we had to hand-restore ~40 doc blocks across 20 files because every
# call after the first lost docs.
#
# Expected fix: when appending to an existing sink, sflit must extract
# the moved decl's leading doc-comment group (every contiguous `//` line
# above the decl, up to the previous decl's closing brace) and re-attach
# it on top of the appended decl.

exec sflit -source big.go -sink merged.go -receiver First -move
exec sflit -source big.go -sink merged.go -receiver Second -move
cmp merged.go expected_merged.go

-- big.go --
package p

// First is the first type — its doc must travel.
type First struct{}

// Second is the second type — its doc must also travel.
type Second struct{}

// Third stays behind.
type Third struct{}
-- expected_merged.go --
package p

// First is the first type — its doc must travel.
type First struct{}

// Second is the second type — its doc must also travel.
type Second struct{}
